questions.question: What security measures does NovaTech implement to protect data at rest and in transit?
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: AES-256 encryption for data at rest; TLS 1.3 for data in transit
questions.source_chunk_content: SECURITY NovaTech implements industry-standard security measures including: AES-256 encryption for data at rest; TLS 1.3 for data in transit SOC 2 Type II certified infrastructure hosted on AWS us-east-1 and us-west-2 Role-based access controls and multi-factor authentication for all internal systems Annual third-party penetration testing and quarterly vulnerability assessments 24/7 security monitoring and incident response team 5. YOUR RIGHTS Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the following rights: Access: request a copy of personal information we hold about you Correction: request correction of inaccurate or incomplete information Deletion: request deletion of your personal information, subject to legal retention obligations Portability: receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format Objection: object to certain processing activities Withdrawal of Consent: withdraw consent at any time where processing is based on consent To exercise your rights, contact privacy@novatech.io or submit a request through the Privacy Portal in your account settings. We will respond within 30 days. 6. INTERNATIONAL DATA TRANSFERS NovaTech operates globally. For transfers of EU/EEA personal data to the United States, we rely on Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission. We are also certified under the EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework. 7. CHILDREN'S PRIVACY The Services are not directed to individuals under 16 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If we become aware of such collection, we will promptly delete the information. 8. CONTACT US Data Protection Officer: dpo@novatech.io Privacy inquiries: privacy@novatech.io | +1 (512) 800-4200 Mailing address: NovaTech Systems, Inc., Attn: Privacy Office, 1200 Innovation Drive, Suite 400, Austin, TX 78701

questions.question: What types of personal data are processed by NovaTech on behalf of the Client?
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: Account info, contact details, usage data, communications, Client-uploaded data
questions.source_chunk_content: DATA PROCESSING AGREEMENT GDPR & CCPA Compliant | Version 2.1 This Data Processing Agreement ("DPA") forms part of and is incorporated by reference into the Master Service Agreement or other agreement between NovaTech Systems, Inc. ("Processor") and the Client ("Controller"). This DPA governs the Processing of Personal Data by NovaTech on behalf of Client. 1. DEFINITIONS Capitalized terms used herein have the meanings given in the MSA or, if not defined therein, as defined in applicable Data Protection Laws, including the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 ("GDPR"), the California Consumer Privacy Act ("CCPA"), and other applicable privacy regulations. 2. PROCESSING DETAILS Element Description Subject matter Processing of Personal Data to provide the NovaTech Services Duration For the Subscription Term and for such further period as specified in the MSA Nature Collection, storage, retrieval, use, disclosure, erasure, and deletion Purpose Provision of Services, analytics, security, fraud prevention Types of Personal Data Account info, contact details, usage data, communications, Client-uploaded data Categories of Data Subjects Client's employees, customers, contractors, and end users 3. OBLIGATIONS OF THE PROCESSOR NovaTech shall: Process Personal Data only on documented instructions from Controller Ensure persons authorized to process are bound by confidentiality obligations Implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures (Article 32 GDPR) Assist Controller in fulfilling data subject rights requests within 30 days Delete or return all Personal Data upon termination as instructed by Controller Provide all information necessary to demonstrate compliance with this DPA 4. SUB-PROCESSORS Controller grants general authorization to engage sub-processors. Current approved sub-processors: Sub-Processor Purpose Location Safeguard Amazon Web Services Cloud infrastructure US, EU SCCs + AWS DPA Snowflake Inc. Data warehousing US SCCs + Snowflake DPA Twilio/SendGrid Transactional email US SCCs + Twilio DPA Datadog, Inc. Monitoring & logging US, EU SCCs + Datadog DPA Stripe, Inc. Payment processing US PCI-DSS + SCCs Zendesk, Inc. Customer support US, EU SCCs + Zendesk DPA 5. SECURITY MEASURES NovaTech maintains the following technical and organizational measures: Encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit Access control: Role-based access, MFA, least privilege principles Audit logging: Comprehensive logs retained for 12 months Vulnerability management: Annual pen tests, quarterly scans, bug bounty program Business continuity: RPO < 1 hour, RTO < 4 hours, geo-redundant backups Certifications: SOC 2 Type II,

questions.question: What is the total budget allocated for the 'Intelligence at Scale' campaign?
questions.source_chunk_content: MARKETING CAMPAIGN BRIEF Campaign: "Intelligence at Scale" | Q1 2025 | Version 1.3 CAMPAIGN OVERVIEW Field Details Campaign Name "Intelligence at Scale" Campaign Type Integrated Demand Generation + Brand Awareness Target Launch January 13, 2025 Campaign Duration 12 weeks (Jan 13 – Apr 6, 2025) Total Budget $1,850,000 Campaign Owner Elena Vasquez, Director of Demand Generation Key Stakeholders CMO (Lena Kovacs), CRO (Carlos Reyes), Product Marketing (3 PMs) CAMPAIGN OBJECTIVES Generate 2,400 MQLs from ICP accounts in Financial Services and Healthcare Drive $58M in new pipeline attributed to campaign Achieve 15M impressions across digital channels with >0.35% CTR Build brand authority: 3 tier-1 media placements, 2 analyst mentions Launch NovaTech Nexus 3.1 with earned media coverage in 8+ publications TARGET AUDIENCE Persona Title Examples Channel Preference Pain Point Focus Primary: Data Leader CDO, VP Data, Head of Analytics LinkedIn, Industry events, Podcasts Team productivity, insight speed Secondary: IT/Security CTO, CISO, VP Engineering Technical blogs, GitHub, Webinars Security, integration, scale Influencer: Finance CFO, VP Finance, FP&A Director Business press, Executive roundtables ROI, cost reduction, compliance CREATIVE CONCEPT "Intelligence at Scale" positions NovaTech as the partner that helps data-driven organizations unlock insights at enterprise scale — without sacrificing security, speed, or sanity. The creative uses the metaphor of a navigation system: NovaTech guides organizations through data complexity to their destination. Key Creative Assets Required Hero video (90 seconds): Customer testimonial + platform demo — Production budget: $180,000 3 pillar content pieces: "State of Enterprise Data 2025" research report, ROI calculator, Compliance automation guide Landing page suite: Campaign hub, 3 persona-specific landing pages, ABM microsites for top 50 accounts Ad creative set: 24 LinkedIn ad variations, 8 Google Display formats, 4 Video ad cuts Email nurture sequence: 5-touch sequence for MQL nurture, 3-touch re-engagement CAMPAIGN BUDGET BREAKDOWN Category Budget % of Total Vendor/Platform Paid Media (LinkedIn, Google, Programmatic) $680,000 36.8% LinkedIn Campaign Manager, Google Ads, 6sense Creative Production $310,000 16.8% Agency: Spark Creative Co. Content & Research $220,000 11.9% Edelman Data & Intelligence (research) Events & Webinars $185,000 10.0% Various venues, ON24 Marketing Technology $145,000 7.8% Demandbase, Drift, Sendoso PR & Analyst Relations $120,000 6.5% Brunswick Group (PR) SDR Enablement & Tools $95,000 5.1% Salesloft, ZoomInfo refresh Contingency (5%) $95,000 5.1% TOTAL $1,850,000 100% CAMPAIGN TIMELINE & MILESTONES

questions.question: What is the contract value for the Vendor Services Contract with CloudBridge Solutions LLC?
questions.source_chunk_content: VENDOR SERVICES CONTRACT Contract No. VSC-2024-0087 PARTIES Party Details Purchaser NovaTech Systems, Inc., 1200 Innovation Drive, Suite 400, Austin, TX 78701 Vendor CloudBridge Solutions LLC, 500 Technology Pkwy, Atlanta, GA 30339 Contract Date October 1, 2024 Contract Value $487,500 (annual) Term October 1, 2024 – September 30, 2026 (2 years with 1-year renewal option) 1. SCOPE OF SERVICES Vendor shall provide NovaTech with the following services as more fully described in Statement of Work No. SOW-2024-0087-A attached hereto: DevOps infrastructure automation and CI/CD pipeline management 24/7 NOC monitoring and Level 1/2 incident response Cloud cost optimization and FinOps advisory services Quarterly infrastructure health assessments and reporting 2. SERVICE LEVEL REQUIREMENTS Metric Target Measurement Method Credit for Breach Incident Response (P1) < 15 minutes Ticketing system timestamps 5% monthly fee Incident Response (P2) < 2 hours Ticketing system timestamps 2% monthly fee Monitoring Uptime 99.95% Automated availability checks 3% monthly fee Report Delivery By 5th business day Submission date 1% monthly fee Quarterly Reviews 100% scheduled Calendar completion 2% monthly fee 3. FEES AND PAYMENT SCHEDULE Service Component Monthly Fee Annual Fee NOC Monitoring (24/7) $15,000 $180,000 DevOps Automation $12,500 $150,000 Cloud FinOps Advisory $6,000 $72,000 Quarterly Health Assessments $2,146 $25,750 (quarterly billing) On-call Premium Support $5,000 $60,000 Total $40,646 $487,750 4. INSURANCE REQUIREMENTS Vendor shall maintain throughout the Term: Commercial General Liability: $2,000,000 per occurrence; $5,000,000 aggregate Professional Liability (E&O): $3,000,000 per claim Cyber Liability: $5,000,000 per incident Workers' Compensation: as required by applicable law NovaTech shall be named as an additional insured on all policies except Workers' Compensation. 5. DATA SECURITY AND COMPLIANCE Vendor acknowledges it may access NovaTech systems and data. Vendor shall comply with NovaTech's Vendor Security Assessment requirements, maintain SOC 2 Type II certification, and execute NovaTech's standard DPA. 6. TERMINATION Either party may terminate for convenience with 90 days written notice. Termination for cause may be immediate upon material breach not cured within 15 days of notice.

questions.question: What is the interest rate that will accrue on overdue amounts according to the payment terms?
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: Overdue amounts will accrue interest at 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is less.
questions.source_chunk_content: (b) resell or sublicense the Services without written consent; (c) use the Services to store or transmit malicious code; or (d) attempt to gain unauthorized access to NovaTech's systems. 3.3 Compliance. Client is solely responsible for ensuring its use of the Services complies with all applicable data protection, privacy, and export laws. 4. FEES AND PAYMENT 4.1 Fees. Client shall pay all fees specified in the applicable Order Form. Fees are non-refundable except as expressly set forth in this Agreement. 4.2 Payment Terms. Unless otherwise specified in the Order Form, all fees are due and payable net 30 days from the invoice date. Overdue amounts will accrue interest at 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is less. 4.3 Taxes. All fees are exclusive of taxes. Client is responsible for all applicable taxes except those based on NovaTech's income. 4.4 Price Adjustments. NovaTech may adjust fees upon renewal with 60 days prior written notice. 5. CONFIDENTIALITY 5.1 Each party agrees to maintain the confidentiality of the other party's Confidential Information using at least the same care it uses to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care. "Confidential Information" means non-public information disclosed by one party to the other that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information. 5.2 Exceptions. The obligations in Section 5.1 do not apply to information that: (a) is or becomes publicly known through no breach of this Agreement; (b) was known prior to disclosure; (c) is received from a third party without restriction; or (d) is required to be disclosed by law or court order. 6. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY 6.1 NovaTech Ownership. NovaTech retains all Intellectual Property Rights in the Services, Documentation, and any improvements or derivative works thereof. No rights are granted to Client except as expressly stated in this Agreement. 6.2 Client Data. As between the parties, Client retains all rights to Client Data. Client grants NovaTech a non-exclusive license to use Client Data solely to provide the Services. 6.3 Feedback. If Client provides feedback or suggestions regarding the Services, NovaTech may use such feedback without restriction or obligation. 7. WARRANTIES AND DISCLAIMERS 7.1 NovaTech warrants that: (a) the Services will

questions.question: What warranties does NovaTech provide regarding the Services?
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: NovaTech warrants that: (a) the Services will perform materially in accordance with the Documentation; (b) it has the right to enter into this Agreement; and (c) it will not knowingly transmit malicious code through the Services.
questions.source_chunk_content: solely to provide the Services. 6.3 Feedback. If Client provides feedback or suggestions regarding the Services, NovaTech may use such feedback without restriction or obligation. 7. WARRANTIES AND DISCLAIMERS 7.1 NovaTech warrants that: (a) the Services will perform materially in accordance with the Documentation; (b) it has the right to enter into this Agreement; and (c) it will not knowingly transmit malicious code through the Services. 7.2 DISCLAIMER. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH HEREIN, THE SERVICES ARE PROVIDED "AS IS" AND NOVATECH MAKES NO OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED, OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR NON-INFRINGEMENT. 8. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY 8.1 IN NO EVENT WILL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, PUNITIVE, OR SPECIAL DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOSS OF PROFITS OR LOSS OF DATA, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES. 8.2 AGGREGATE LIABILITY. EACH PARTY'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT WILL NOT EXCEED THE AMOUNTS PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT IN THE TWELVE MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM. 9. TERM AND TERMINATION 9.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until all Order Forms expire or are terminated. 9.2 Either party may terminate this Agreement for material breach if the breach is not cured within 30 days of written notice. 9.3 NovaTech may suspend Services immediately if Client's account is 60 or more days past due. 9.4 Upon termination, NovaTech will make Client Data available for export for 30 days, after which it may be deleted. 10. GENERAL PROVISIONS 10.1 Governing Law. This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict of law principles. 10.2 Dispute Resolution. Any disputes will be resolved by binding arbitration in Austin, Texas, administered by AAA under its Commercial Arbitration Rules. 10.3 Entire Agreement. This Agreement, including all exhibits and Order Forms, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding its subject matter. 10.4 Amendment. This Agreement may only be amended by a written instrument signed by both parties. 10.5 Assignment. Neither party may assign this Agreement without prior written consent, except in connection with a merger or acquisition. SIGNATURE PAGE NovaTech Systems, Inc. Client Authorized Signature _______________________ _______________________ Printed Name Sarah J. Mitchell Title Chief Legal Officer

questions.question: What is the total net income forecasted for FY2025?
What is the base salary for the position of Senior Software Engineer at NovaTech Systems, Inc.?
questions.source_chunk_content: November 18, 2024 Ms. Priya Raghunathan 4820 Sunrise Court, Austin, TX 78745 OFFER OF EMPLOYMENT Dear Priya, On behalf of NovaTech Systems, Inc., I am delighted to offer you the position of Senior Software Engineer, Platform Engineering, reporting to Jordan Chen, VP of Platform Engineering. We were impressed by your skills and experience and believe you will make a significant contribution to our team. Position Details Term Details Position Senior Software Engineer — Platform Engineering Department Engineering — Platform & Infrastructure Location Austin, TX (Hybrid — 2 days in-office) Start Date January 6, 2025 Reports To Jordan Chen, VP Platform Engineering FLSA Status Exempt Compensation Package Component Amount / Details Base Salary $178,000 per year, paid bi-weekly Annual Bonus Target 15% of base salary ($26,700), based on individual and company performance Signing Bonus $15,000 (50% on start date; 50% at 6 months; clawback if < 1 year) RSU Grant 4,000 RSUs vesting over 4 years (1-year cliff, quarterly thereafter) Remote Work Stipend $500 one-time setup + $100/month internet Benefits You will be eligible for all NovaTech employee benefits starting on your first day of employment, including: Medical, dental, and vision insurance (100% employee premium covered) 401(k) with 6% employer match, immediately vested Unlimited PTO policy (minimum 15 days recommended annually) 16 weeks fully paid parental leave $2,500 annual learning and development budget Life insurance (2x base salary) and short/long-term disability Conditions of Employment This offer is contingent upon: Successful completion of a background verification check Execution of NovaTech's Employee Invention Assignment and Confidentiality Agreement (EIACA) Verification of your eligibility to work in the United States (Form I-9) Confirmation of your current employment references This offer letter does not constitute a contract of employment. Your employment will be at-will. Please indicate your acceptance by signing below and returning a copy to me by November 25, 2024. We are very excited about the prospect of you joining NovaTech! Warmly, Monica Alvarez Senior Talent Acquisition Partner | NovaTech Systems, Inc. monica.alvarez@novatech.io | (512) 800-4250 ACCEPTANCE I, Priya Raghunathan, accept the offer of employment described above and confirm that all information provided during the hiring process is accurate. Candidate Signature Date _______________________ _______________________

questions.question: What is the 2025 target for Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) growth at NovaTech Systems, Inc.?
questions.source_chunk_content: GO-TO-MARKET STRATEGY 2025 NovaTech Systems, Inc. | Confidential | Approved by CMO December 2024 STRATEGIC CONTEXT NovaTech enters 2025 at a pivotal moment. After 7 consecutive quarters of 30%+ ARR growth, we face intensifying competition from Databricks and Microsoft Fabric while also seeing greenfield opportunity in financial services compliance automation and manufacturing IoT analytics. Our GTM must be precise, targeted, and measurable. 2025 GO-TO-MARKET OBJECTIVES Objective 2024 Baseline 2025 Target Owner ARR Growth $186M ARR $252M ARR (+35%) CRO + CMO Net New Logos (Enterprise) 67 95 Enterprise Sales NRR (Net Revenue Retention) 118% 125% Customer Success Pipeline Coverage 3.2x 4.0x Marketing + SDR CAC Payback Period 22 months 18 months Finance + Marketing Brand NPS 42 55 Marketing MQL to SQL Conversion 14% 22% Marketing + Sales Ops TARGET MARKET SEGMENTATION Segment TAM 2025 Focus Key Message Financial Services $8.2B Primary — 45% of pipeline Compliance-ready data intelligence Healthcare & Life Sciences $4.7B Primary — 30% of pipeline HIPAA-secure unified data platform Manufacturing & Industrial $3.9B Growth — 15% of pipeline OT/IT convergence, predictive analytics Government & Education $2.1B Emerging — 10% of pipeline FedRAMP, cost efficiency MESSAGING ARCHITECTURE Primary Value Proposition NovaTech Nexus is the only data intelligence platform purpose-built for regulated industries — delivering insights 10x faster than legacy approaches while automating compliance workflows that consume 60% of your data team's time. Pillar Messages by Persona Persona Primary Fear Core Message Proof Point CDO / VP Data Team drowning in data prep instead of insights Reclaim 60% of data team time with intelligent automation FinServ customer: 58% reduction in data prep CFO Can't justify data investments without ROI clarity Average 347% 3-year ROI with payback in 14 months Meridian Financial case study CTO / CISO Security, compliance risk of new vendor SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, FedRAMP Moderate — most certified platform in category Trust Center at trust.novatech.io CIO Integration complexity, vendor sprawl One platform that works with your existing stack; 200+ pre-built connectors Integration Marketplace stats DEMAND GENERATION PLAN Channel 2025 Budget Pipeline Target Key Programs Paid Digital (LinkedIn, Google) $2,800,000 $42M pipeline Account-based ads, thought leadership Field Events & Conferences $1,900,000 $31M pipeline DataSummit 2025, Money20/20, HIMSS Content & SEO $850,000 $18M pipeline Research reports, data leader interviews Partner

questions.question: What is the recommended minimum number of days for the unlimited PTO policy?
questions.source_chunk_content: 2x annual salary, company-paid Day 1 PTO Unlimited PTO policy; minimum 15 days recommended Day 1 Parental Leave 16 weeks fully paid for primary caregiver; 8 weeks for secondary After 6 months Learning Budget $2,500 per year for training, conferences, and certifications After 90 days Equity (RSUs) Eligible roles receive RSUs with 4-year vest, 1-year cliff Offer-specific Remote Work Stipend $500 home office setup + $100/month internet stipend Day 1 Mental Health Free access to Calm app, Headspace, and EAP program Day 1 WORKPLACE POLICIES Work Hours and Flexibility NovaTech operates on a hybrid-first model. Most roles require in-office presence 2 days per week (Tuesday and Thursday are anchor days). Core hours are 10am–3pm local time. Outside core hours, employees have flexibility to structure their workday. Code of Conduct All employees are expected to act professionally, ethically, and with integrity. Prohibited conduct includes: Harassment, discrimination, or bullying of any kind Misuse of Company assets or confidential information Falsification of records or expense reports Unauthorized disclosure of proprietary information Conflicts of interest not disclosed to HR Reporting Violations Report concerns to your manager, HR, or anonymously through the EthicsLine at 1-888-555-0102 or ethics@novatech.io. Retaliation against good-faith reporters is strictly prohibited. PERFORMANCE AND DEVELOPMENT Performance Management Cycle NovaTech uses a continuous feedback model supplemented by quarterly check-ins and an annual review: Q1: Goal setting and alignment (January–March) Q2: Mid-year progress review (April–June) Q3: Ongoing feedback and development conversations (July–September) Q4: Annual performance assessment and merit calibration (October–December) Performance Ratings Rating Description Merit Multiplier 5 - Exceptional Consistently exceeds all expectations; top 5% 1.5x–2.0x 4 - Exceeds Regularly exceeds expectations; top 20% 1.2x–1.5x 3 - Meets Fully meets expectations; solid contributor 1.0x–1.2x 2 - Developing Partially meets; improvement plan required 0x–0.5x 1 - Unsatisfactory Does not meet expectations; PIP in place 0x

questions.question: What types of information are included in the definition of Confidential Information under the Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement?
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: Confidential Information includes, without limitation: Business plans, financial projections, and strategic roadmaps Technical specifications, source code, and software architectures Customer lists, pricing structures, and contractual terms Personnel information, compensation data, and organizational structure Marketing strategies, product roadmaps, and competitive analyses Research and development projects and scientific data
questions.source_chunk_content: NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT MUTUAL — CONFIDENTIAL This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of _____________ ("Effective Date") by and between NovaTech Systems, Inc. ("NovaTech") and _________________________ ("Counterparty") (collectively, the "Parties"). 1. PURPOSE The Parties wish to explore a potential business relationship (the "Purpose") and, in connection with that exploration, may disclose certain confidential and proprietary information to each other. This Agreement governs all such disclosures. 2. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION "Confidential Information" means any information disclosed by one Party ("Disclosing Party") to the other ("Receiving Party"), whether orally or in writing, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure. Confidential Information includes, without limitation: Business plans, financial projections, and strategic roadmaps Technical specifications, source code, and software architectures Customer lists, pricing structures, and contractual terms Personnel information, compensation data, and organizational structure Marketing strategies, product roadmaps, and competitive analyses Research and development projects and scientific data 3. OBLIGATIONS 3.1 The Receiving Party shall: (a) hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent; (c) use it solely for the Purpose; and (d) protect it with at least the same degree of care used to protect its own confidential information, but not less than reasonable care. 3.2 Access shall be limited to employees and contractors who have a need to know for the Purpose and who are bound by written confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those in this Agreement. 4. EXCEPTIONS Obligations do not apply to information that: (a) is or becomes publicly known through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) was already known prior to disclosure; (c) is received from a third party without restriction; (d) is independently developed without use of Confidential Information; or (e) must be disclosed by law, regulation, or court order (with prompt written notice to the Disclosing Party). 5. TERM This Agreement is effective as of the Effective Date and expires three (3) years thereafter, unless extended by mutual written agreement. Obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term survive for an additional three (3) years following the expiration or termination of this Agreement. 6. RETURN OF INFORMATION Upon

questions.question: What is the ideal customer profile for the primary ICP in terms of industry and revenue?
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: Industry Financial Services, Healthcare, Manufacturing Revenue $500M+ annual revenue
questions.source_chunk_content: ENTERPRISE SALES PLAYBOOK Q1 2025 Edition | NovaTech Systems | Confidential PLAYBOOK OVERVIEW This playbook guides Enterprise Account Executives through NovaTech's proven sales methodology from initial discovery through signed contract. Follow this process to drive consistent, predictable revenue while delivering genuine value to prospects. IDEAL CUSTOMER PROFILE (ICP) Attribute Primary ICP Secondary ICP Industry Financial Services, Healthcare, Manufacturing Retail, Insurance, Government Revenue $500M+ annual revenue $100M–$499M annual revenue Employee Count 1,000+ employees 250–999 employees Data Complexity 5+ disconnected data sources 3–5 data sources Buyer CDO, CTO, CFO as primary; CIO as influencer VP Data, VP Engineering, VP Finance Deal Size (ACV) $200K–$2M+ $75K–$199K Sales Cycle 90–180 days 45–90 days SALES STAGES AND CRITERIA Stage Name Entry Criteria Exit Criteria Target Days 1 Prospecting Account identified in CRM ICP validated, initial interest confirmed — 2 Discovery Meeting accepted Pain confirmed, budget range validated, champion identified 0–14 3 Technical Validation Champion engaged Technical win confirmed, IT signed off 14–45 4 Business Case Economic buyer engaged ROI model approved by champion 30–60 5 Proposal EB confirmed investment range Verbal agreement on pricing structure 15–30 6 Negotiation Proposal accepted in principle All business and legal issues resolved 15–30 7 Closed Won Contract signed — — DISCOVERY FRAMEWORK — THE 5 WHYs Business Pain Questions Why is your current analytics process creating problems? (Surface emotional pain) Why hasn't this been solved before? (Identify obstacles, previous failures) Why is solving this important now? (Establish urgency and timing) Why would your organization prioritize this investment? (Connect to strategic goals) Why would NovaTech be a better fit than building in-house or using competitor X? MEDDPICC Qualification Framework Element What to Discover Red Flags Metrics Quantified impact of the problem; ROI expectations No clear success metrics defined Economic Buyer Who controls the budget and signs contracts Unknown or inaccessible EB Decision Criteria Technical, security, vendor, and commercial requirements Criteria heavily favor a competitor Decision Process Approval workflow, legal process, procurement timeline Procurement process undefined Paper Process Legal, security review, and procurement timelines Legal blackout periods approaching Identify Pain Specific, quantified business problem with urgency Pain is vague or not urgent Champion Internal advocate with access to EB and skin in the game No champion or champion without credibility Competition Alternatives being evaluated, build vs.

questions.question: What must employees do to retain ownership of specific prior inventions before starting their employment at NovaTech?
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: Employees who wish to retain ownership of specific prior inventions must disclose them in writing before commencing employment.
questions.source_chunk_content: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY POLICY Effective Date: January 1, 2024 | Internal Document 1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE This Intellectual Property Policy establishes guidelines for the ownership, protection, and appropriate use of intellectual property created by or for NovaTech Systems, Inc. This Policy applies to all employees, contractors, consultants, and advisors. 2. OWNERSHIP OF WORK PRODUCT 2.1 Company Ownership. All inventions, innovations, works of authorship, software, designs, processes, know-how, and improvements (collectively, "Work Product") created by employees within the scope of employment are owned by NovaTech. This includes work performed outside normal business hours if: (a) it uses Company resources; (b) it relates to the Company's current or reasonably anticipated business; or (c) it results from work performed for the Company. 2.2 Assignment. Each employee and contractor shall execute an Employee Invention Assignment and Confidentiality Agreement (EIACA) as a condition of engagement. 2.3 Prior IP. Employees who wish to retain ownership of specific prior inventions must disclose them in writing before commencing employment. NovaTech will review and acknowledge such disclosures within 30 days. 3. PATENT POLICY 3.1 Employees are encouraged to disclose potentially patentable inventions to the IP Committee via the Invention Disclosure Form available on the intranet. 3.2 NovaTech will evaluate each disclosure within 60 days and decide whether to pursue patent protection. If NovaTech elects not to pursue protection, it may assign or license rights to the inventor. 3.3 Inventors of granted patents may receive recognition bonuses as specified in the Inventor Recognition Program. 4. TRADEMARK USAGE Mark Type Registration Status Usage Guidelines NovaTech Systems Word mark US Reg. 5,847,291 Always use in full on first reference NovaTech Abbreviated US Reg. 5,847,292 Acceptable after first full reference NovaTech Nexus Product name Pending Use only with ™ symbol NT Logo (blue gradient) Design mark US Reg. 6,012,440 Do not alter colors or proportions 5. OPEN SOURCE SOFTWARE 5.1 Use of open source software in Company products or services requires prior written approval from the Engineering IP Review Board. 5.2 Copyleft licenses (GPL, AGPL, LGPL) require legal review before use. Permissive licenses (MIT, Apache 2.0, BSD) are generally acceptable. 5.3 An inventory of all approved open source components is maintained in the Open Source Registry on the engineering wiki. 6. THIRD-PARTY IP Employees must not incorporate third-party copyrighted

questions.question: What is the projected ROI of the NovaTech Nexus Enterprise solution over three years?
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: a projected ROI of 347% over three years.
questions.source_chunk_content: NovaTech Systems, Inc. ENTERPRISE SOLUTION PROPOSAL Prepared for: Meridian Financial Group | November 2024 EXECUTIVE SUMMARY Meridian Financial Group ("Meridian") faces a critical challenge: as data volumes grow 40% year-over-year, your analytics infrastructure struggles to deliver insights at the speed the business demands. Finance teams wait 3–5 days for reports that should take hours, and compliance teams lack real-time visibility into risk exposure. NovaTech Systems proposes NovaTech Nexus Enterprise — a unified data intelligence platform that will reduce your reporting latency from days to minutes, automate 80% of compliance data workflows, and deliver a projected ROI of 347% over three years. UNDERSTANDING YOUR CHALLENGES Pain Point Current State Business Impact Report generation speed 3–5 days for weekly risk reports Delayed decisions, regulatory risk Data silos 12 disconnected data systems Inconsistent data, manual reconciliation Compliance automation 75% manual data gathering $2.1M annual compliance labor cost Scalability Peak load causes system slowdowns Business disruption during quarter-end Data governance No unified data catalog Audit findings and remediation costs PROPOSED SOLUTION NovaTech Nexus Enterprise — Core Modules NovaTech DataSync: Real-time integration with all 12 of your source systems, including Bloomberg, Calypso, and your core banking platform NovaTech Reporting Engine: Self-service analytics with sub-60-second refresh on dashboards supporting 500+ concurrent users NovaTech Compliance Suite: Automated CCAR, DFAST, and MiFID II reporting with full audit trails NovaTech Data Catalog: AI-powered data discovery and governance across all connected sources NovaTech Sentinel: Real-time data quality monitoring with proactive anomaly detection COMMERCIAL PROPOSAL Component Year 1 Year 2 Year 3 Platform License (1,000 users) $840,000 $882,000 $926,100 NovaTech Compliance Suite add-on $240,000 $252,000 $264,600 Implementation & Migration Services $175,000 — — Training & Change Management $45,000 $15,000 $15,000 Premier Support (24/7, dedicated CSM) $72,000 $75,600 $79,380 TOTAL $1,372,000 $1,224,600 $1,285,080 Less: Multi-year discount (15%) ($205,800) ($183,690) ($192,762) NET TOTAL $1,166,200 $1,040,910 $1,092,318 ROI ANALYSIS Value Driver Annual Value Methodology Compliance labor reduction (80%) $1,680,000 Current $2.1M cost x 80% automation Faster decision-making impact $950,000 CFO estimate: 5 deals improved per year Avoided regulatory penalties $500,000 Based on industry penalty benchmarks IT infrastructure consolidation $320,000 4 legacy system decommissions Analyst productivity gains $280,000 60 analysts x 25% time savings x avg cost TOTAL ANNUAL VALUE $3,730,000 3-Year Net Benefit $8,205,772 After total investment ROI 347%

questions.question: What is the employment status of employees at NovaTech Systems?
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: Employment with NovaTech is at-will, meaning either the employee or the Company may terminate the employment relationship at any time, with or without cause, and with or without notice, subject to applicable law.
questions.source_chunk_content: NovaTech Systems, Inc. EMPLOYEE HANDBOOK 2024 Edition | Version 5.0 WELCOME TO NOVATECH Welcome to NovaTech Systems! We are thrilled to have you join our team of over 1,200 innovators across 14 countries. NovaTech is a leading B2B SaaS company helping enterprises transform their data operations through intelligent automation. This handbook outlines the policies, benefits, and expectations that shape our workplace. Our Mission To empower every enterprise with the tools to turn data complexity into competitive advantage — securely, intelligently, and at scale. Our Core Values Integrity First: We do the right thing, even when it's hard Customer Obsession: We exist to create value for our customers Relentless Innovation: We challenge assumptions and embrace change One Team: We win together through collaboration and mutual respect Ownership Mindset: We act like owners and take accountability for outcomes EMPLOYMENT POLICIES Equal Opportunity Employment NovaTech is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic. At-Will Employment Employment with NovaTech is at-will, meaning either the employee or the Company may terminate the employment relationship at any time, with or without cause, and with or without notice, subject to applicable law. Probationary Period All new employees complete a 90-day onboarding and probationary period. During this time, performance will be evaluated and employment may be terminated with or without cause. COMPENSATION & BENEFITS Salary and Pay Cycles Employees are paid bi-weekly via direct deposit. Pay stubs are available through the Workday HR portal. Salary ranges are set based on market data benchmarked annually against Radford and Mercer surveys. Performance Reviews and Merit Increases Annual performance reviews occur in January. Merit increases are effective February 1. The Company targets a merit pool of 4-6% of payroll, distributed based on individual performance ratings. Benefits Summary Benefit Details Eligibility Health Insurance Medical (BCBS), Dental, Vision — 100% employee premium covered Day 1 401(k) 6% company match, immediate vesting Day 1 Life Insurance 2x annual salary, company-paid Day 1 PTO Unlimited PTO policy; minimum 15 days recommended Day 1 Parental Leave 16 weeks fully paid for primary caregiver; 8 weeks for secondary After 6 months Learning Budget $2,500 per year

questions.question: What does the term 'Services' refer to in the Master Service Agreement?
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: "Services" means the software-as-a-service platform, APIs, data analytics tools, and related professional services provided by NovaTech as described in any applicable Order Form or Statement of Work ("SOW").
questions.source_chunk_content: NovaTech Systems, Inc. 1200 Innovation Drive, Suite 400, Austin, TX 78701 MASTER SERVICE AGREEMENT This Master Service Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of the Effective Date set forth below, by and between NovaTech Systems, Inc., a Delaware corporation ("NovaTech" or "Company"), and the entity identified below ("Client"). 1. DEFINITIONS 1.1 "Services" means the software-as-a-service platform, APIs, data analytics tools, and related professional services provided by NovaTech as described in any applicable Order Form or Statement of Work ("SOW"). 1.2 "Client Data" means all data, content, and information submitted by Client or its users to the Services. 1.3 "Documentation" means the user manuals, technical specifications, and online help materials made available by NovaTech. 1.4 "Intellectual Property Rights" means all patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, and other proprietary rights. 1.5 "Order Form" means a mutually executed ordering document that specifies the Services, fees, and subscription term. 2. SERVICES 2.1 Provision of Services. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, NovaTech will provide Client with access to the Services during the Subscription Term set forth in the applicable Order Form. 2.2 Support. NovaTech will provide Client with technical support in accordance with the Support Policy attached hereto as Exhibit A. Standard support is included with all subscriptions; Premium and Enterprise support tiers are available at additional cost. 2.3 Availability. NovaTech will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain Service availability of at least 99.9% uptime per calendar month, excluding scheduled maintenance windows communicated at least 48 hours in advance. 2.4 Updates. NovaTech reserves the right to modify, update, or discontinue features of the Services with reasonable notice to Client, provided that material reductions in functionality will be communicated at least 90 days in advance. 3. CLIENT OBLIGATIONS 3.1 Account Security. Client is responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of login credentials and for all activities that occur under Client's accounts. 3.2 Acceptable Use. Client shall use the Services only in accordance with this Agreement and all applicable laws and regulations. Client shall not: (a) reverse engineer or decompile the Services; (b) resell or sublicense the Services without written consent; (c) use the Services to store or transmit malicious code; or (d) attempt to gain unauthorized access to NovaTech's systems. 3.3 Compliance. Client is solely responsible for ensuring

questions.question: What should employees do if they suspect IP infringement or policy violations?
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: Report suspected IP infringement or policy violations to legal@novatech.io.

THIRD-PARTY IP Employees must not incorporate third-party copyrighted materials, trade secrets, or patented technology without appropriate licenses. Violations may result in disciplinary action and personal liability. 7. REPORTING AND COMPLIANCE Report suspected IP infringement or policy violations to legal@novatech.io. The Company will investigate all reports and maintain confidentiality to the extent possible.

questions.question: What are the minimum home office requirements for employees working remotely at NovaTech Systems, Inc.?
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: Employees working remotely must maintain a workspace that meets the following minimum standards: Reliable high-speed internet connection (minimum 25 Mbps download, 10 Mbps upload) Dedicated, private workspace free from disruptive noise during meetings Company-issued equipment used for all work activities Use of NovaTech-approved VPN for all access to Company systems Physical security measures to prevent unauthorized access to screens or documents
questions.source_chunk_content: REMOTE WORK POLICY Policy No. HR-POL-2024-011 | Effective: March 1, 2024 1. PURPOSE This policy establishes guidelines for remote work arrangements at NovaTech Systems, Inc. NovaTech embraces a hybrid-first work model that combines the flexibility of remote work with the collaboration benefits of in-person interaction. 2. ELIGIBILITY Remote work is available to full-time employees whose job responsibilities can be performed effectively outside the office. Eligibility is determined by role classification: Work Arrangement In-Office Requirement Eligible Roles Hybrid (Standard) 2 days/week (Tue & Thu) Software Engineers, PMs, Designers, Analysts Hybrid (Flexible) 1 day/week minimum Senior IC and above with manager approval Fully Remote Occasional travel only Approved remote-first roles outside commute range On-Site Required 5 days/week IT Operations, Facilities, select Customer Success 3. HOME OFFICE REQUIREMENTS Employees working remotely must maintain a workspace that meets the following minimum standards: Reliable high-speed internet connection (minimum 25 Mbps download, 10 Mbps upload) Dedicated, private workspace free from disruptive noise during meetings Company-issued equipment used for all work activities Use of NovaTech-approved VPN for all access to Company systems Physical security measures to prevent unauthorized access to screens or documents 4. STIPENDS AND EQUIPMENT Item Amount Process Home office setup (one-time) $500 Submit receipts via Concur within 60 days of start Internet reimbursement (monthly) $100 Auto-applied to bi-weekly paycheck Ergonomic assessment At cost (covered) Request through HR portal Equipment refresh (every 3 years) Standard laptop + peripherals IT request ticket 5. AVAILABILITY AND COMMUNICATION STANDARDS Be available and responsive during core hours: 10am–3pm local time Maintain calendar accuracy with daily availability windows marked Respond to Slack messages within 4 hours during core hours Attend all required team meetings via video (camera on encouraged) Notify manager same-day if unable to meet availability commitments 6. INTERNATIONAL REMOTE WORK Employees wishing to work from outside the United States for more than 30 consecutive days must submit a request to HR and Legal at least 60 days in advance. Approval depends on tax nexus implications, visa requirements, and data security compliance. 7. POLICY REVIEW This policy is reviewed annually by HR in consultation with Legal, IT Security, and Executive Leadership. The next scheduled review is January 2025.
